tangamangaBanner.title: Tangamanga Park
tangamangaBanner.description: The most important green lung of San Luis Potosí, with over 420 hectares of green areas, artificial lakes, sports courts, and trails to enjoy nature.
tangamangaBanner.subtitle: Zoo · Planetarium · Museum · Japanese Garden
tangamangaBanner.altText: Parque Tangamanga I — zoo, trails and lake in San Luis Potosí

tangamanga.title: Tangamanga Park I
tangamanga.subtitle: Mexico's Second Largest Urban Park - Where Nature Meets Adventure

history.title: History & Cultural Significance
history.meaningText: The name "Tangamanga" comes from the Huachichil language, an indigenous group that once inhabited the region. It translates to "meeting place" or "place of gathering", reflecting the park's role as a central social and cultural hub for the community.
history.originText: The park was formed on the grounds of the old Hacienda de la Tenería, which dates back to 1609. The hacienda's main building is still preserved within the park, serving as a silent witness to over four centuries of Potosino history.
history.creationText: The project's first phase began in 1982, when the land was expropriated and over a thousand trees were planted. The park was officially inaugurated in 1983 during the government of Prof. Carlos Jonguitud Barrios. In 1997, the government gave it the official name "Parque Carlos Jonguitud Barrios", although most people simply know it as Tangamanga Park.
history.impactText: With an area of 420 hectares, Tangamanga Park I is positioned as the second largest urban park in Mexico, only surpassed by Bosque de Chapultepec in Mexico City, and one of the largest urban parks in the world. Its more than 1.8 million trees make it the most important green lung of San Luis Potosí, producing 800 tons of oxygen per year.

attractions.natural1: Botanical garden with endemic and exotic species
attractions.natural3: Walking and cycling trails over 10 km long
attractions.cultural1: Zoo with native and exotic species
attractions.cultural4: Tangamanga EcoMuseum in historic tannery buildings

biodiversity.title: Biodiversity & Nature
biodiversity.text: The park hosts an extraordinary diversity of flora and fauna. In its 420 hectares, more than 1.8 million native and exotic trees coexist, along with migratory and resident birds, as well as small mammals and reptiles. The botanical garden is especially important for the conservation of endemic species from the Potosino region and the Mexican desert.

sustainability.title: Self-Sustainability
sustainability.description: Tangamanga Park is a model of environmental sustainability
sustainability.feature1Text: Water treatment plants that allow water reuse for irrigation of all green areas
sustainability.feature2Text: Own production of organic compost to nourish the park's vegetation
sustainability.feature3Text: Generation of 800 tons of oxygen per year thanks to its 1.8 million trees

tips.tip1: Bring sunscreen, hat, and plenty of water
tips.tip2: Wear comfortable shoes for walking - the park is very large
tips.tip3: Arrive early on weekends to find good parking
tips.tip4: Check the Teatro Carlos Amador event calendar
tips.tip5: Visit the EcoMuseum to learn about the Hacienda de la Tenería history
tips.tip6: The park closes Monday afternoons - plan your visit accordingly
tips.tip8: Don't leave trash - help keep the city's green lung clean

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Parque Tangamanga
faq.q1: Is Parque Tangamanga free to enter?
faq.a1: Yes. Parque Tangamanga I is completely free to enter, including the zoo, the Laberinto de las Ciencias (interactive science museum), the botanical garden, and all walking/running trails. Some attractions like the planetarium and aquarium have small fees ($20-50 MXN).
faq.q2: What are the hours for Parque Tangamanga?
faq.a2: Monday: 5 AM to 11 AM (maintenance day). Tuesday to Saturday: 5 AM to 10:30 PM. Sunday: 5 AM to 6 PM. The zoo opens Tuesday to Sunday 9 AM to 5 PM.
faq.q3: What can you do at Parque Tangamanga?
faq.a3: Free zoo with 100+ species, planetarium, aquarium (Splash), Laberinto de las Ciencias science museum, Japanese garden, botanical garden, open-air theater, lakes for pedal boats, running and cycling trails, sports courts, picnic areas, and bike rental.
faq.a4: Yes. The park is well-patrolled during opening hours. Avoid visiting alone after dark on weekdays. Weekend mornings are the busiest and safest time.
faq.q5: How big is Parque Tangamanga compared to other parks?
faq.a5: Parque Tangamanga I covers 411 hectares, larger than Central Park in New York (341 ha). It has over 400,000 trees.
faq.q6: When is the BMW Tangamanga Marathon?
faq.a6: The Maratón Internacional Tangamanga runs every year on the last Sunday of June. Edition 40 takes place June 28, 2026, starting and ending at Parque Tangamanga I. Registration opens in January each year.
faq.q7: Is there parking at Parque Tangamanga?
faq.a7: Yes. Free parking is available at multiple entrances. The main lots are near the zoo (east entrance) and near the theater (north entrance). On weekends and holidays the lots fill before 11 AM — arrive early or use rideshare.
faq.q8: How do I get to Parque Tangamanga by public transit?
faq.a8: The park sits on Av. Dr. Salvador Nava. Several city bus routes stop at the main entrances: Ruta 23, 32, and 52 reach the east side (zoo). Rideshare and taxis drop visitors at any of the 8 gates.
faq.q9: What can kids do at Parque Tangamanga?
faq.a9: Splash aquarium, the free zoo, Laberinto de las Ciencias (science museum), paddle boats on the lake, the Japanese garden, open theater performances on weekends, bike rentals, and multiple playgrounds. Most activities are free.
faq.q10: What's the difference between Tangamanga I and Tangamanga II?
faq.a10: Tangamanga I (this page) is the 411-hectare tourist park with the zoo, planetarium, aquarium, and gardens. Tangamanga II is a 189-hectare sports park built over SLP's old airport — focused on archery, running tracks, auto racing, and soccer fields. Both are free and both are in San Luis Potosí city limits.
faq.q11: Is Parque Tangamanga bigger than Central Park?
faq.a11: Yes. Parque Tangamanga I covers 411 hectares (1,015 acres), which is 70 hectares larger than New York's Central Park (341 ha / 843 acres). It is the second-largest urban park in Mexico.
faq.q12: How long does it take to visit Parque Tangamanga?
faq.a12: Plan at least 3 to 4 hours for the main attractions (zoo, Japanese garden, lakes). A full visit including the planetarium, Splash aquarium, and Laberinto de las Ciencias takes a full day. Bring water and sunscreen — the park's altitude (1,864 m) and open layout make sun exposure significant.
faq.q13: Are dogs allowed at Parque Tangamanga?
faq.a13: Yes, dogs are allowed on leash in the public walking areas of Parque Tangamanga I. Dogs are not permitted inside the zoo, planetarium, or aquarium. For an off-leash experience, Parque Tangamanga II has a dedicated fenced pet zone.
faq.q14: Can I bring food to Parque Tangamanga?
faq.a14: Yes. Picnics are welcome in the designated lawn areas. Glass containers and alcohol are not permitted. There are also food vendors and small restaurants inside the park, most active on weekends.
faq.q15: What is the best time to visit Parque Tangamanga?
faq.a15: Weekend mornings (8 AM to noon) offer the best experience: cool temperatures, active zoo animals, open food stalls, and free weekend events at the open-air theater. Weekday mornings are quietest. Avoid Monday if you want full access — most attractions close for maintenance Monday afternoons.

maraton.subtitle: The city's biggest running event takes place right here in Tangamanga I every year. Over 17,000 runners, four certified distances (5K, 10K, half marathon, full marathon), and a forty-year tradition.

comparison.title: Parque Tangamanga I vs Tangamanga II
comparison.description: San Luis Potosí has two Tangamanga parks. They share a name and a philosophy, but they serve different visitors.
comparison.t1Desc: The iconic park. Zoo, planetarium, aquarium, Japanese garden, theater, lakes, and 400,000 trees. Best for families, tourists, and first-time visitors.
comparison.t2Desc: Built over the city's old airport. Sports-focused: archery, auto racing track, soccer fields, basketball, tennis, pet zone and kids area. Best for athletes and serious runners.

quickAnswer.title: Parque Tangamanga I at a glance
quickAnswer.definition: Parque Tangamanga I is a 411-hectare public urban park in San Luis Potosí, Mexico. It is the second-largest urban park in Mexico and is 70 hectares larger than New York's Central Park. The park opened in 1980 and is operated by CECURT (Centro Cultural Universitario Rafael del Villar). Entry to the park, the zoo, the Laberinto de las Ciencias, and the gardens is free of charge.
keyFacts: Size: 411 hectares (1,015 acres) — #2 urban park in Mexico
Admission: free (planetarium and aquarium charge $20–50 MXN)
Hours: Tuesday–Saturday 5 AM–10:30 PM · Sunday 5 AM–6 PM · Monday 5–11 AM (maintenance)
Location: Av. Dr. Salvador Nava, southeast San Luis Potosí
Operator: CECURT (Centro Cultural Universitario Rafael del Villar)
Main attractions: zoo, planetarium, Splash aquarium, Laberinto de las Ciencias, Japanese garden, botanical garden, open-air theater, lakes

centroHistorico.title: Historic Downtown
centroHistorico.subtitle: The Colonial Heart of San Luis Potosí - Where History Comes Alive

intro.title: Welcome to the Heart of San Luis Potosí
intro.paragraph1: The Historic Downtown is much more than a collection of old buildings: it's the living soul of the city. Every plaza, every alley, every pink quarry facade tells a story dating back over four centuries.
intro.paragraph2: Here, past and present coexist in perfect harmony. While admiring the majesty of colonial architecture, you can savor specialty coffee in an 18th-century courtyard, explore contemporary art galleries, or enjoy the city's finest gastronomy.

history.title: History & Heritage
history.originsText: Founded in 1592, San Luis Potosí was born thanks to the region's mining wealth. The Historic Downtown preserves the essence of over four centuries of history, with baroque, neoclassical, and colonial buildings that narrate the grandeur of New Spain.
history.architectureText: The Historic Downtown is an open-air museum with over 1,500 catalogued historic buildings. The pink quarry stone, characteristic of the city, adorns the facades of temples, mansions, and government buildings. Every street reveals architectural treasures that transport you to another era.
history.todayText: Far from being a frozen museum, the Historic Downtown pulses with life. Cafés in colonial courtyards, contemporary art galleries in 18th-century buildings, and gourmet restaurants in old mansions create a unique fusion between past and present.

gastronomy.title: Gastronomy & Nightlife
gastronomy.intro: The Historic Downtown is a gastronomic paradise where tradition and innovation meet. From century-old cantinas to author cuisine restaurants, every corner offers a memorable culinary experience.

nightlife.title: Entertainment & Nightlife
nightlife.text: When the sun sets, the Historic Downtown transforms. The plazas light up, street musicians appear, and bars open their doors. From concerts at Teatro de la Paz to jazz nights in colonial courtyards, downtown offers entertainment for all tastes. On weekends, pedestrian streets fill with life through cultural events, food fairs, and artistic performances.
nightlife.tourText: One of the best nightlife experiences is exploring the bar zone on foot. Within a few blocks you'll find:
nightlife.spot1: El Ricardito - Best vinyl and music bar in town, small but incredible vibes
nightlife.spot2: Rockabilly - Rock music and retro atmosphere
nightlife.spot3: Las Bóvedas - Unique space in historic architecture
nightlife.tourTip: All within walking distance, perfect for a safe and fun bar crawl.

shopping.title: Shopping & Crafts
shopping.artisansText: The Historic Downtown is the best place to find authentic regional crafts. From the famous Santa María del Río rebozo to high-quality leatherwork, each piece tells a story of artisanal tradition.
shopping.item1: Santa María del Río Rebozos - Century-old textile tradition
shopping.item2: Leatherwork and leather goods - Belts, bags, saddles
shopping.item3: Traditional sweets - Queso de tuna, charamuscas, glorias
shopping.item4: Ceramics and talavera - Unique decorative pieces

hiddenGems.title: Downtown Secrets
hiddenGems.intro: Beyond the tourist sites, the Historic Downtown holds secrets that only locals know. Here we reveal some of our favorites.
hiddenGems.gem1Text: A hidden passage with art galleries and bohemian cafés. Perfect for a quiet afternoon of exploration.
hiddenGems.gem2Text: Many buildings preserve spectacular interior courtyards. Enter to admire fountains, gardens, and intimate architecture.
hiddenGems.gem3Text: At sunset, the pink quarry stone glows with warm tones. The best time for photography at Plaza de Armas.
hiddenGems.gem4Text: Streets close to traffic. Families, cyclists, and pedestrians take over the streets in a festive atmosphere.

practical.title: Practical Information
practical.gettingThereText: Uber and taxi are the most comfortable options. Downtown is very walkable once you arrive.
practical.parkingText: Several public parking lots around downtown. Avoid pedestrian streets.
practical.safetyInfoText: The Historic Downtown is one of the safest areas in the city, with constant patrol and good lighting.

tips.title: Visitor Tips
tips.tip1: Wear comfortable shoes - streets are cobblestone
tips.tip2: Visit on weekdays to avoid crowds
tips.tip3: Sundays have fewer cars and more pedestrian life
tips.tip4: Bring cash for markets and street vendors
tips.tip5: Ask locals for recommendations - they're very friendly
tips.tip6: Explore the alleys - that's where the hidden gems are

funFacts.title: Fun Facts
funFacts.fact1: The Historic Downtown has over 1,500 buildings catalogued as historical heritage.
funFacts.fact2: The pink quarry stone that adorns the buildings is extracted from local quarries and is unique to Mexico.
funFacts.fact3: Benito Juárez established Mexico's capital here in 1863 during the French intervention.
funFacts.fact4: Teatro de la Paz was inaugurated in 1894 and is considered one of the most beautiful theaters in Latin America.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: When and where is Cara Sucia Country Fest 2026?
faq.a1: Saturday June 13, 2026 at La Legendaria, located at Lib. Sur Anillo Perif. 720, Garita de Jalisco, 78294 San Luis Potosí, S.L.P. Doors open at 2:00 PM.
faq.q2: How much are tickets and where do I buy them?
faq.a2: Tickets are sold in phases (Phase 1 $380, Phase 2 $480, Phase 3 $580 MXN). Sold exclusively on Superboletos. Buy early for the best price.
faq.q3: Is this a family event? Can kids attend?
faq.a3: Yes, it's a family-friendly festival. Kids under 5 enter free accompanied by an adult. There's a dedicated kids zone with activities for the little ones.
faq.q4: Will there be food at the festival?
faq.a4: Yes. Cara Sucia BBQ serves its smoked menu throughout the event, plus guest food trucks and a full bar with cocktails, beer, wine and alcohol-free options.
faq.q5: What bands or artists are performing?
faq.a5: Check the official lineup poster on this page for the full bill. For updates, follow @soycarasucia on Instagram.
faq.q6: Can I wear my cowboy hat, boots and western outfit?
faq.a6: Absolutely — full country dress code is welcome. There's also an on-site Hat Customization Bar if you want to personalize your hat at the festival.
faq.a7: Yes, parking is available at La Legendaria. We recommend arriving early or using Uber/DiDi during peak hours.
faq.a8: The event is mostly outdoors. Light rain doesn't stop the show; bring a poncho or umbrella. Follow @soycarasucia for any weather updates.

hero.title: Tangamanga Marathon
hero.subtitle: BMW International Marathon — 40th Edition
hero.description: One of Mexico's oldest and most prestigious road races celebrates 40 years. Run through Tangamanga Park and the Historic Center of San Luis Potosí in 4 distances.
hero.location: San Luis Potosí, capital of the state of San Luis Potosí, Mexico

distances.title: Race Categories & Distances
distances.subtitle: Choose your distance and get ready to run one of Mexico's most iconic races.

fiveK.name: 5K Fun Run
fiveK.description: Ideal for beginners and families. Route within Tangamanga Park.

tenK.name: 10K
tenK.description: Mixed route through the park and surrounding streets. Age and gender categories.

halfMarathon.name: Half Marathon (21K)
halfMarathon.description: FMAA-certified course through Tangamanga Park, Av. Salvador Nava and Historic Center.

marathon.name: Marathon (42K)
marathon.description: Full World Athletics & FMAA-certified course. The most iconic route in SLP.

route.title: Route Map
route.subtitle: The course crosses San Luis Potosí's most iconic landscapes.
route.description: The route starts at Tangamanga Park I, goes up Av. Salvador Nava toward the Historic Center, passes Plaza de los Fundadores and the Cathedral, then returns to the park. The full marathon repeats a modified loop.
route.hydration: Hydration points every 2.5 km with water and sports drink.
route.medical: Medical stations at km 5, 10, 15, 21, 30 and finish line.
route.landmarks: Landmarks: Tangamanga Park, Av. Carranza, Plaza de Armas, Metropolitan Cathedral, Teatro de la Paz.

runnerGuide.title: Runner's Guide
runnerGuide.subtitle: Essential tips for running in San Luis Potosí.
runnerGuide.altitudeDesc: San Luis Potosí sits at 1,860 meters (6,100 ft) above sea level. If you're coming from a low-altitude city, arrive at least 2 days early to acclimatize. Altitude affects your pace and recovery.
runnerGuide.weatherDesc: Temperature between 15°C (59°F) at start and 28°C (82°F) at midday. Possible afternoon showers. Wear light clothing, a cap and sunscreen. Hydrate more than usual.
runnerGuide.hydrationDesc: At this altitude, you need more water than usual. Drink 500ml 2 hours before start. Take advantage of every hydration point on the course.
runnerGuide.warmupDesc: The warm-up zone is at the main esplanade of Tangamanga Park. Arrive at least 45 minutes before your start time.

kit.title: Runner's Kit
kit.subtitle: Everything you receive and need to know for packet pickup.
kit.includesList: Official 40th anniversary shirt, race bib number, timing chip, finisher medal (upon completion), runner's bag with sponsor products
kit.pickupDesc: Marathon Expo at Tangamanga Park. Friday June 26 and Saturday June 27, 10:00 AM to 7:00 PM. Bring your registration confirmation and official ID.
kit.docsDesc: Registration confirmation (printed or digital), valid government-issued ID, sports medical certificate (required for 21K and 42K).

spectators.title: Spectator Info
spectators.subtitle: Support your runner from the best viewing spots along the course.
spectators.viewingDesc: Finish line at Tangamanga Park, Av. Salvador Nava (km 8-10), Plaza de Armas in Historic Center (km 15), Av. Carranza (km 18-20).
spectators.parkingDesc: Parking available at Tangamanga Park and surrounding streets. Arrive early — spots fill up fast on race day.
spectators.activitiesDesc: Tangamanga Park has green areas, a lake, playgrounds and cafés. The Historic Center is 15 minutes away.

accommodation.title: Accommodation & Transport
accommodation.airportDesc: SLP International Airport (BJX) is 25 minutes from Tangamanga Park. Taxis and shuttle service available.
accommodation.busDesc: The Central Bus Station is 20 minutes from the park. Direct connections from Mexico City, Monterrey, Guadalajara and Querétaro.
accommodation.hotelsDesc: Tangamanga Sur area: hotels 5-10 min from the park. Historic Center: charming colonial hotels 15 min away. Book in advance — June is peak season due to the marathon.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Registration usually opens in January. Check maratontangamanga.com for exact dates and prices by category. Prices increase as the event approaches.
faq.q2: Do I need a medical certificate?
faq.a2: Yes, for 21K and 42K distances a valid sports medical certificate is required (issued within the last 6 months) when picking up your kit.
faq.a3: Yes, the marathon has official pacemakers for 3:30, 4:00, 4:30 and 5:00 hours in the marathon, and 1:45 and 2:00 in the half marathon.
faq.a4: Marathon: 6 hours. Half marathon: 3 hours. 10K: 1 hour 30 minutes. 5K: 45 minutes.
faq.a5: Yes. At 1,860m above sea level, expect 5-10% lower performance than at sea level. Arrive 2-3 days early to acclimatize and adjust your target pace.
faq.q6: Can I change distance after registering?
faq.a6: Distance changes are subject to availability and allowed up to 2 weeks before the event, with a possible fee adjustment.
faq.a7: Yes, there is a bag check service at the start/finish area in Tangamanga Park. Avoid leaving valuables.
faq.q8: How do I check my results?
faq.a8: Results are posted in real-time on the marathon website and official app. The timing chip records split and net times.
faq.a9: Yes, every 2.5 km there are hydration stations with water, sports drink, and at some points, fruit (banana and orange).
faq.a10: Yes, headphones are allowed, but keeping the volume low is recommended so you can hear instructions from marshals and emergency vehicles.

sim.title: Get a Mexican SIM card
sim.description: Telcel (best coverage), AT&T, or Movistar. Available at OXXO or carrier stores. $150-300 MXN.

apps.title: Download essential apps
apps.description: Uber, DiDi (rides), Rappi (delivery), Google Maps, WhatsApp (primary messaging).

atm.title: Withdraw pesos from ATM
atm.description: Use ATMs inside banks (Santander, BBVA). Decline conversion to home currency.

cash.title: Keep cash on hand
cash.description: Many places are cash-only. Keep $500-1,000 MXN for daily needs.

translate.title: Download Google Translate
translate.description: Get the Spanish offline pack. Camera feature helps with menus and signs.

inm.title: INM address registration
inm.description: Required within 90 days if you have residency. Bring passport, residency card, proof of address.

curp.title: Get your CURP
curp.description: Mexico's population ID. Free at Registro Civil with passport and residency card.

rfc.title: Obtain RFC (if working)
rfc.description: Tax ID for employment. Apply at SAT office with CURP, passport, proof of address.

consulate.title: Register at your consulate
consulate.description: For emergency assistance and voting abroad. Most done online.

license.title: Mexican driver's license (optional)
license.description: Apply at Secretaría de Seguridad Pública. Cost: ~$800-1,200 MXN.

electricity.title: Set up electricity (CFE)
electricity.description: Transfer account at CFE office with lease and ID. Pay at OXXO or online.

water.title: Set up water (INTERAPAS)
water.description: Transfer at INTERAPAS office. Bills typically $150-400 MXN/month.

gas.title: Arrange gas delivery
gas.description: Providers: Gas Express Nieto, Zeta Gas. Stationary tanks refilled, portable exchanged.

internet.title: Set up internet
internet.description: Telmex (most coverage), Totalplay (fiber), Izzi. Plans $400-800 MXN/month.

garrafon.title: Buy water garrafones
garrafon.description: Tap water NOT drinkable. Buy 5-gallon jugs from Bonafont, Ciel. ~$30-40 MXN each.

dispenser.title: Get a garrafón dispenser
dispenser.description: Available at Soriana, Walmart, Amazon Mexico. $500-2,000 MXN.

bank.title: Open Mexican bank account
bank.description: BBVA, Santander (expat-friendly), Banorte. Need: passport, residency, CURP, proof of address.

mercadoPago.title: Set up Mercado Pago
mercadoPago.description: Mexico's popular digital wallet. Works at many stores, transfers to friends, pay bills.

spei.title: Learn SPEI transfers
spei.description: Mexico's instant bank transfer system. Free, fast, widely used for rent and services.

international.title: Set up international transfers
international.description: Wise (best rates), Remitly, or wire transfer for regular transfers.

bills.title: Understand bill payments
bills.description: Pay at OXXO (24/7), bank apps, online portals. OXXO is easiest for most services.

doctor.title: Find a general doctor
doctor.description: Ask in expat groups for English speakers. Consultations: $400-800 MXN.

dentist.title: Find a dentist
dentist.description: Dental care is excellent and affordable. Cleaning: $400-800 MXN.

pharmacy.title: Locate pharmacies
pharmacy.description: Farmacias Guadalajara, Farmacias del Ahorro. Many meds available without prescription.

insurance.title: Set up health insurance
insurance.description: IMSS (~$6,000 MXN/year) or private: GNP, Seguros Monterrey, Bupa.

facebook.title: Join expat Facebook groups
facebook.description: Search "Expats in San Luis Potosí". Great for advice and meeting others.

exchange.title: Find language exchange partners
exchange.description: Practice Spanish, help with English. Check Meetup, university boards, cafes.

classes.title: Enroll in Spanish classes
classes.description: UASLP offers courses. Private tutors: $150-300 MXN/hour.

neighborhoods.title: Explore neighborhoods
neighborhoods.description: Walk Centro Histórico, Parque Tangamanga, Lomas. Best way to feel at home.

events.title: Attend local events
events.description: Check our events calendar. FENAPO (August) is the biggest annual fair.

neighbors.title: Meet your neighbors
neighbors.description: A simple "Buenos días" goes a long way. Neighbors are valuable for recommendations.

rides.title: Download ride apps

hero.title: FENAPO 2026
hero.subtitle: Feria Nacional Potosina
hero.description: Mexico's most traditional fair returns to San Luis Potosí with concerts, gastronomy, culture and entertainment for the whole family. Full lineup: Katy Perry, Mötley Crüe, Bizarrap, Grupo Firme, Los Tigres del Norte, Gloria Trevi and many more.
hero.location: San Luis Potosí, capital of the state of San Luis Potosí, Mexico

about.title: What is FENAPO?
about.p1: The Feria Nacional Potosina (FENAPO) is Mexico's most important and traditional fair. Held every year during August in San Luis Potosí, it coincides with the patron saint festivities honoring San Luis Rey de Francia (August 25), the city's patron saint.
about.p2: With over half a century of history, FENAPO brings together culture, entertainment, gastronomy and commerce at the Recinto Ferial, a 30+ hectare venue that transforms for nearly a month into the center of Potosino social life.
about.p3: The fair generates over 2 billion pesos in economic impact and attracts more than 3 million visitors each edition, making it the state's most important tourism event.

highlights.title: What you'll find at FENAPO:
highlights.item1: Foro de las Estrellas with national and international artists (free entry)
highlights.item2: Palenque with the best of regional Mexican music (ticketed)
highlights.item3: Gastronomy zone with Potosino, Mexican and international cuisine
highlights.item4: Commercial exhibition with local and national products
highlights.item7: Cultural pavilion with art and handicrafts

about.organizer: Organized annually by the Government of the State of San Luis Potosí, FENAPO is one of the largest state fairs in Mexico.

artists.title: Full Lineup
artists.subtitle: Official FENAPO 2026 lineup: 21 free nights at the Foro de las Estrellas and 14 nights at the Palenque (separate ticket), August 7–30.
artists.freeNotice: Foro de las Estrellas concerts, mechanical rides, parking, and fair entry will be free this year per the Governor of San Luis Potosí.

areas.title: Fair Areas
areas.subtitle: The fairgrounds are divided into zones so you can find exactly what you're looking for.

teatro.name: Foro de las Estrellas
teatro.desc: Main stage (formerly Teatro del Pueblo) with top-tier artists. Free concerts every night.

palenque.name: Palenque
palenque.desc: Premium shows with the best of Mexican and regional music.

gastro.name: Gastronomy Zone
gastro.desc: Potosino, Mexican and international food. Enchiladas, gorditas, asado de boda and more.

commercial.name: Commercial Exhibition
commercial.desc: Local and national products and services. Exclusive fair deals and promotions.

rides.name: Mechanical Rides
rides.desc: Roller coaster, Ferris wheel, bumper cars and more fun for the whole family.

cultural.name: Cultural Pavilion
cultural.desc: Art exhibitions, handicrafts, dance and traditional Potosino music.

livestock.name: Livestock Exhibition
livestock.desc: Cattle exhibition, charreadas and ranching traditions of San Luis Potosí.

kids.name: Kids Zone
kids.desc: Activities and shows designed for the youngest members of the family.

visitorGuide.title: Visitor Guide
visitorGuide.subtitle: Everything you need to know to make the most of your FENAPO visit.
visitorGuide.bestDaysDesc: Monday to Thursday is less crowded and easier to get around. Fridays and Saturdays offer the best shows but expect crowds. Sundays are ideal for families.
visitorGuide.bringDesc: Comfortable walking shoes, sun protection, cash (some vendors don't accept cards), portable phone charger.
visitorGuide.safetyDesc: The venue has private security and police presence. Watch your belongings in crowded areas. Information and first aid stations are available.
visitorGuide.familyDesc: The kids zone and rides are ideal for children. Avoid Fridays and Saturdays if bringing young children due to crowds.

gettingThere.title: Getting There
gettingThere.address: Av. Fco. Martínez de la Vega No. 255, San Luis Potosí
gettingThere.byCarDesc: Ample parking inside and outside the venue. From downtown, take Av. Salvador Nava south (~15 min). Expect traffic during peak fair hours.
gettingThere.byTransitDesc: Bus routes: 10, 12, 17. Special transport services operate during the fair. Taxis and Uber available.
gettingThere.byPlaneDesc: SLP Airport is 20 minutes away. Taxis available at the terminal.
gettingThere.distanceDesc: The venue is 15 minutes from the Historic Center via Av. Salvador Nava.

accommodation.title: Accommodation
accommodation.description: August is SLP's busiest month due to FENAPO. Book in advance.
accommodation.nearbyDesc: Hotels along Av. Salvador Nava and Tangamanga Sur, 5-10 minutes from the venue.
accommodation.centroDesc: Boutique and colonial hotels downtown, 15 minutes away. Ideal if you want to explore the city beyond the fair.
accommodation.budgetDesc: Hotel prices rise during FENAPO. Booking 2-3 months ahead can save you up to 40%.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: When are the exact dates for FENAPO 2026?
faq.a1: FENAPO 2026 runs August 7–30, 2026 at the Recinto Ferial in San Luis Potosí, with concerts every night at the Foro de las Estrellas and the Palenque.
faq.q2: How much do Palenque tickets cost?
faq.a2: Palenque prices vary by artist and zone: General $700–$1,300 MXN, Dorada $1,100–$3,600, VIP $1,700–$4,600, Diamante $1,900–$5,500. Tickets are sold via SLP Fast Ticket (slpfastticket.com) and at the Palenque box office. Popular acts like Grupo Firme and Emmanuel & Mijares sell out quickly.
faq.a3: Yes. In 2026 entry to the fairgrounds, the Foro de las Estrellas concerts, mechanical rides and parking are all free by the Governor's decree. Only the Palenque requires a separate ticket.
faq.a4: Yes, parking is available inside the venue and in nearby lots. Cost varies. Arrive early on weekends to find a spot.
faq.q5: Can I bring food into the venue?
faq.a5: Outside food and drinks are not allowed. There's a large gastronomy zone with options for all budgets.
faq.a6: Generally 11:00 AM to 12:00 AM (midnight) Monday-Thursday, and 11:00 AM to 2:00 AM Friday-Saturday. Hours may vary.
faq.a7: Yes. The venue has private security, police presence and assistance stations. As with any mass event, watch your belongings and avoid carrying unnecessary valuables.
faq.a8: No, pets are not allowed inside the fairgrounds for safety and animal welfare reasons.
faq.a9: Yes, there are ATMs inside the venue. However, lines can be long. Bringing enough cash is recommended.
faq.a10: Yes, FENAPO is a family event. There's a kids zone, children's rides and family shows. Sundays are usually the best days to go with children.
faq.a11: No formal dress code. Casual and comfortable clothing. Closed shoes are recommended for the venue terrain. For Palenque, people tend to dress up more.
faq.q12: Is it accessible for people with disabilities?
faq.a12: The venue has access ramps and designated areas. The Foro de las Estrellas and Palenque have accessible sections. Arriving early is recommended to secure a spot.

cta.title: Don't Miss FENAPO 2026!
cta.description: Mexico's most traditional fair. Nearly a month of culture, entertainment, gastronomy and fun for the whole family in San Luis Potosí.

preFenapo.title: Pre-FENAPO Events at the Recinto Ferial
preFenapo.subtitle: The fairgrounds are already hosting events ahead of the main August celebration. A great way to experience the venue before the crowds.
preFenapo.event1Desc: Regional norteño competition featuring local and touring accordion acts.
preFenapo.event2Desc: Regional Mexicano double bill at the Recinto Ferial.
preFenapo.event3Desc: Mexico's biggest potosino metal festival. Full coverage on our event page.
preFenapo.event4Desc: International volleyball tournament at the fair pavilions.

quickAnswer.title: FENAPO 2026 at a glance
quickAnswer.definition: FENAPO 2026 — Mexico's Feria Nacional Potosina — takes place August 7–30, 2026 at the Recinto Ferial on Av. Fco. Martínez de la Vega, San Luis Potosí. The full lineup features 35 artists: 21 free nights at the Foro de las Estrellas (Katy Perry, Mötley Crüe, Bizarrap, Gloria Trevi, Los Tigres del Norte, Sin Bandera, Kenia Os and more) and 14 ticketed nights at the Palenque (Grupo Firme, Julión Álvarez, Edén Muñoz, Ha*Ash, Emmanuel & Mijares and more). Fair entry, mechanical rides, parking and Foro de las Estrellas concerts are free. Palenque tickets are sold via SLP Fast Ticket.

mcpPage.faq1q: Do I need an API key to use the MCP server?

mcpPage.faq2q: What data can I access through MCP?

mcpPage.faq3q: Does it work with clients other than Claude Desktop?

mcpPage.faq4q: How is this different from the CLI?
mcpPage.faq4a: The MCP server integrates directly into your AI assistant so you can ask questions in natural language. The CLI is for terminal scripts and automation. Both access the same data.

meta.title: Digital Nomad Guide to San Luis Potosí | Remote Work in Mexico
meta.description: Everything digital nomads need to know about San Luis Potosí: coworking spaces, internet speeds, cost of living, visa options, neighborhoods and lifestyle.

hero.title: Work Remotely from San Luis Potosí
hero.subtitle: Affordable living, fast internet, rich culture — discover why SLP is Mexico's best-kept secret for digital nomads.

whySlp.title: Why San Luis Potosí for Remote Work?
whySlp.intro: San Luis Potosí combines affordability, safety, culture and connectivity in a way few Mexican cities can match. Central timezone (CST/CDT), direct flights to the US, and a cost of living 60-70% lower than comparable US cities.

timezone.title: US-Aligned Timezone
timezone.description: CST/CDT — same as Chicago, Dallas and Houston. Perfect for US-based clients and teams.

safety.title: Safe & Walkable
safety.description: One of Mexico's safest mid-size cities. The historic center and residential areas are well-maintained and walkable.

cost.title: Low Cost of Living
cost.description: Live comfortably on $1,000-$1,500 USD/month. Enjoy a quality of life that would cost 3x in the US.

culture.title: Rich Cultural Scene
culture.description: Baroque architecture, year-round festivals, world-class gastronomy and a welcoming local community.

coworking.title: Coworking Spaces & WiFi Cafes
coworking.intro: SLP has a growing number of work-friendly cafes and coworking spaces with reliable WiFi, power outlets and comfortable seating. Most offer day passes or monthly memberships.

internet.title: Internet & Connectivity
internet.intro: Fiber optic internet is widely available in SLP. Most neighborhoods have access to 100+ Mbps, and many cafes offer 50-100 Mbps. Mobile data coverage (4G/5G) is strong with Telcel, AT&T Mexico and Movistar.

costOfLiving.title: Cost of Living Breakdown
costOfLiving.intro: San Luis Potosí is one of the most affordable cities in central Mexico. Here's a realistic monthly budget for a digital nomad living comfortably.

visas.title: Visa Options for Nomads
visas.intro: Mexico is one of the easiest countries for remote workers. You don't need a work visa to work remotely for a foreign employer — the key distinction is that you're not employed by a Mexican company.

neighborhoods.title: Best Neighborhoods for Nomads
neighborhoods.intro: Your neighborhood choice depends on your lifestyle. Here are the top three for remote workers.

lomas.name: Lomas
lomas.description: Modern, safe, walkable. Close to restaurants, gyms and malls. Popular with expats and professionals.

centro.name: Centro Histórico
centro.description: Colonial charm, cafes at every corner, cultural venues. Best for those who want to be in the heart of the action.

tangamanga.name: Tangamanga Area
tangamanga.description: Quiet, green, near Mexico's largest urban park. Great for runners, cyclists and nature lovers.

healthcare.title: Healthcare for Nomads
healthcare.intro: Private healthcare in SLP is affordable and high-quality. A doctor's visit costs $30-$50 USD, and major hospitals like Hospital Lomas and Hospital Angeles have English-speaking staff.

lifestyle.title: Life Outside Work
lifestyle.intro: SLP offers plenty to do on your off-hours — from hiking and weekend getaways to museums and live music.

meta.title: Visit San Luis Potosí — Travel Guide | Things to Do & See
meta.description: Plan your trip to San Luis Potosí, Mexico. Top attractions, 48-hour itinerary, food guide, day trips to Huasteca Potosina, and practical travel tips.

hero.title: Visit San Luis Potosí
hero.subtitle: Colonial charm, natural wonders and world-class gastronomy — discover one of Mexico's most beautiful and authentic cities.

attractions.title: Top Attractions
attractions.intro: San Luis Potosí's historic center is a treasure trove of baroque and neoclassical architecture, grand plazas and world-class museums.
attractions.centroDesc: Walk through colonial plazas, baroque churches, and pedestrian streets. The heart of the city and a candidate for UNESCO World Heritage status.
attractions.tangamangaDesc: One of Mexico's largest urban parks with 57 hectares of trails, lakes, sports facilities and botanical gardens.
attractions.museumsDesc: From the Museo Nacional de la Máscara to the Centro de las Artes — SLP has over 20 museums to explore.
attractions.historyDesc: Discover 400+ years of history from colonial silver mining to the Mexican Revolution.

day1.title: Day 1 — Historic Center & Culture
day1.morning: Start at Plaza de Armas, visit the Cathedral and Palacio de Gobierno. Coffee at a terrace cafe on Plaza del Carmen.
day1.afternoon: Museo Nacional de la Máscara, walk Calle Zaragoza, explore Plaza de los Fundadores and Templo del Carmen.
day1.evening: Dinner at a traditional Potosino restaurant. Try enchiladas potosinas and asado de boda.

day2.title: Day 2 — Parks, Markets & Food
day2.morning: Parque Tangamanga for a morning walk or run. Visit the botanical garden and lake.
day2.afternoon: Mercado Hidalgo for street food and local shopping. Head to Calzada de Guadalupe for the scenic walk.
day2.evening: Explore the cantina scene or catch live music. End with mezcal at a rooftop bar.

food.title: Food & Dining
food.intro: Potosino cuisine is one of Mexico's best-kept culinary secrets. From enchiladas potosinas to gorditas and craft mezcal, the food scene is vibrant and affordable.

dayTrips.title: Day Trips & Weekend Getaways
dayTrips.intro: Some of Mexico's most spectacular natural wonders are within a few hours of SLP.

huasteca.name: Huasteca Potosina
huasteca.description: Turquoise waterfalls, jungle rivers, and surreal natural pools. Mexico's adventure paradise.

realDeCatorce.name: Real de Catorce
realDeCatorce.description: Ghost town turned magic pueblo. Arrive through a 2.3km tunnel into a mystical desert landscape.

xilitla.name: Xilitla & Las Pozas
xilitla.description: Edward James' surrealist garden in the jungle. A dreamlike sculpture park hidden in the Sierra Madre.

culture.title: Cultural Experiences
culture.intro: SLP has one of Mexico's richest cultural calendars, from colonial-era festivals to contemporary art.

byAir.title: By Air
byAir.description: Ponciano Arriaga International Airport (SLP) has direct flights from Mexico City (VivaAerobus, Volaris), Monterrey, Dallas, Houston and Chicago. The airport is 15 minutes from downtown.

byBus.title: By Bus
byBus.description: First-class buses (Primera Plus, ETN) connect SLP to Mexico City (4-5 hrs), Querétaro (3 hrs), Guadalajara (4 hrs) and Monterrey (5 hrs). The central bus station is modern and well-connected.

bestTime.title: Best Time to Visit
bestTime.intro: SLP has a semi-arid climate with warm days and cool nights. The city sits at 1,850m elevation, keeping temperatures comfortable year-round.

octDec.title: Oct – Dec
octDec.description: Ideal weather (18-25°C). Día de Muertos celebrations, holiday markets, and Festival de Luz.

janMar.title: Jan – Mar
janMar.description: Cool and dry (12-22°C). Carnival in February, Holy Week processions in March/April.

aprJun.title: Apr – Jun
aprJun.description: Warm (22-32°C). Festival de San Luis in June, spring cultural season.

julSep.title: Jul – Sep
julSep.description: Rainy season, afternoon showers (20-28°C). FENAPO — the biggest festival — in August.

seo.title: Parque Tangamanga II: SLP's Sports Park Guide 2026
seo.description: 189 ha built over SLP's old airport. Archery, auto racing track, sports courts, pet zone, running loops. Hours, directions, what to do — complete guide.
seo.keywords: parque tangamanga 2, parque tangamanga ii, tangamanga ii san luis potosi, pista tangamanga, tiro con arco tangamanga, parque deportivo slp

tangamanga2.title: Parque Tangamanga II
tangamanga2.subtitle: San Luis Potosí's sports park — 189 hectares over the city's old airport. Archery, auto racing, running tracks, pet zone.

quickFacts.title: Quick Facts
quickFacts.location: South-west SLP, over the old airport runway
quickFacts.address: Av. Salvador Nava Martínez, Fracc. Tangamanga

history.title: A Park Built on a Runway
history.body: Parque Tangamanga II opened in 1985 on the site of San Luis Potosí's original municipal airport. When the state relocated the airport to its current location on the Mexico City highway, the old runway and terminal apron were reclaimed as an urban green space dedicated to sport and recreation. The long, flat geometry of the former runway made it ideal for running loops, cycling circuits, and an auto racing track — features that define Tangamanga II's identity to this day.

attractions.title: Main Attractions
attractions.archeryDesc: Home to Club de Tiro con Arco Tangamanga, one of Mexico's most active archery venues. Open practice fields plus coaching for beginners and competitors.
attractions.racingDesc: Asphalt racing circuit hosting regional and national series events. The only permanent racing venue in the city.
attractions.sportsDesc: Soccer fields, basketball courts, tennis courts, volleyball, and a running track. Free to use, first-come first-served.
attractions.petDesc: Fenced off-leash area with agility equipment. One of few dog-friendly zones of its size in central Mexico.
attractions.kidsDesc: Playgrounds, open lawns for games, and seasonal family events. Popular for weekend birthday parties.
attractions.runningDesc: Flat, shaded loops of 1km, 2.5km, and 5km using the former runway and perimeter paths. Favorite among local marathoners.

compare.title: Should I visit Tangamanga I or Tangamanga II?
compare.body: If it's your first time in San Luis Potosí, go to Tangamanga I — the bigger park with the zoo, planetarium, and gardens. Pick Tangamanga II if you're running, training a sport, bringing a pet, or watching a motorsport event.

faq.title: FAQ — Parque Tangamanga II
faq.a1: Yes. Entry to the park and use of the public courts and running loops is free. Some specific clubs (archery academy, auto racing events) charge their own fees.
faq.a2: Tuesday to Sunday 5:30 AM to 7 PM (last entry 6 PM). Monday 5:30–11 AM only — reduced hours for maintenance.
faq.q3: Where exactly is Parque Tangamanga II?
faq.a3: South-west San Luis Potosí, off Av. Salvador Nava Martínez. It was built over the old municipal airport, so it sits on what used to be the runway and terminal apron.
faq.q4: Can I do archery at Tangamanga II?
faq.a4: Yes. Club de Tiro con Arco Tangamanga offers open practice and beginner lessons. Contact them directly via their Facebook page for current schedule and rates.
faq.q5: Is it safe to run at Tangamanga II at dawn?
faq.a5: Yes. Tangamanga II is popular with runners precisely because it's well-lit and monitored from 5:30 AM. Weekend mornings are the busiest and safest.
faq.q6: Is Parque Tangamanga II bigger or smaller than Tangamanga I?
faq.a6: Tangamanga II (189 ha) is smaller than Tangamanga I (411 ha). But Tangamanga II is still the second-largest green space in San Luis Potosí city and is dedicated specifically to sports and athletics.
faq.q7: What sports can I do at Parque Tangamanga II?
faq.a7: Tangamanga II offers: archery (Club de Tiro con Arco Tangamanga), auto racing (permanent circuit), soccer, basketball, tennis, volleyball, running, cycling, and walking. Most public courts are free; specialty clubs (archery academy, racing events) charge their own fees.
faq.q8: How do I get to Parque Tangamanga II by car or transit?
faq.a8: By car: Av. Salvador Nava Martínez, Fracc. Tangamanga, postal code 78260. Free parking at multiple gates. By transit: city bus routes serving Av. Salvador Nava stop near the main gates; rideshare drops visitors at any entrance.

quickAnswer.title: Parque Tangamanga II at a glance
quickAnswer.definition: Parque Tangamanga II is a 189-hectare sports-focused public park in San Luis Potosí, Mexico. It was built in 1985 on the site of the city's original municipal airport. The park specializes in sports infrastructure — archery, auto racing, running loops, soccer fields, tennis, basketball, and a dedicated off-leash pet zone. Entry is free.
